title: oxytropis leucantha var. gaspensis
intro: Oxytropis leucantha var. gaspensis is a rare and endangered variant of the Oxytropis leucantha plant, specifically found in the Gaspé Peninsula of Quebec, Canada. It is a perennial legume belonging to the pea family, known for its distinctive white and pink flowers. The roots of Oxytropis leucantha var. gaspensis have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as sore throats and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being by promoting digestion, reducing fever, and boosting the immune system.
